latest.title: FY26 Full Year Results
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 4587I
Zegona Communications PLC
16 June 2026
FY26 Full Year Results
16 June 2026
ZEGONA COMMUNICATIONS PLC
FY26 Full Year Results
Zegona publishes:
·
its Annual Report for the financial year ended 31 March 2026 which is available on Zegona's website
:
https://www.zegona.com/investor-relations/annual-report-and-accounts.aspx
·
Vodafone Spain's FY26 results presentation. This presentation is for
professional investors and is available at
:
https://www.zegona.com/investor-relations/results-and-presentations/rp-2026.aspx
Zegona announces that it will hold its Annual General Meeting ("AGM") at 9.00 am BST on Thursday 30 July 2026. A Circular to Shareholders containing the AGM Notice has been posted today. Details on each of the Resolutions to be put forward at the AGM are set out in the Circular.
A copy of the Annual Report and
the AGM
Notice
have been submitted to the Financial Conduct Authority's National Storage Mechanism and will shortly be available for inspection at
https://data.fca.org.uk/#/nsm/nationalstoragemechanism
.
This information contains regulated information as per 6.3.7R of the Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules of the Financial Conduct Authority.
Investor Inquiries:
Media Inquiries:
Alfonso Enriquez
Jamie De Andres
Tilly Abraham (Sodali&Co)
Info@zegona.com
jamie.andres@vodafone.com
zegona@info.sodali.com
About Zegona
Zegona is publicly listed on the Main Market of the LSE. It was established in 2015 with the objective of investing in businesses in the European Telecommunications, Media and Technology sector and improving their performance to deliver attractive shareholder returns. Zegona is led by former Virgin Media executives Eamonn O'Hare and Robert Samuelson. In 2024, Zegona completed the acquisition of Vodafone Spain.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. Terms and conditions relating to the use and distribution of this information may apply. For further information, please contact
rns@lseg.com
or visit
www.rns.com
.RNS may use your IP address to confirm compliance with the terms and conditions, to analyse how you engage with the information contained in this communication, and to share such analysis on an anonymised basis with others as part of our commercial services. For further information about how RNS and the London Stock Exchange use the personal data you provide us, please see our
Privacy Policy
.
END
FR EQLBFQQLXBBF

Zegona Communications plc
Total Voting Rights
Zegona hereby notifies that its capital as of 31 May 2026 comprised 226,163,802 ordinary shares of 1 pence each. Zegona holds no shares in treasury and the total voting rights in Zegona as of 31 May 2026 were 226,163,802.
The above figure of 226,163,802 should be used by shareholders (and others with notification obligations) as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, Zegona under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

About Zegona
Zegona is publicly listed on the Main Market of the LSE. It was established in 2015 with the objective of investing in businesses in the European Telecommunications, Media and Technology sector and improving their performance to deliver attractive shareholder returns. Zegona is led by former Virgin Media executives Eamonn O'Hare and Robert Samuelson. In 2024, Zegona completed the acquisition of Vodafone Spain.

company_30s.what_they_do: Zegona builds and manages telecom infrastructure – fibre networks, cell towers and related assets – that provide connectivity for mobile operators and large enterprises.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company sits in a growing market where demand for high‑speed data is rising. Its network assets give it a moat against new entrants and it can earn recurring revenue from long‑term contracts with operators and cloud providers.
company_30s.current_state: Zegona is a mid‑cap listed firm that has been generating steady cash flows and paying regular dividends. It has recently been reshaping ownership and governance, which has helped it secure a spot in a major global equity index. The share price is low relative to its asset base, suggesting a potential bargain.

rns_narrative: The company has been tightening its ownership structure – buying back shares, clarifying voting rights and reporting its holdings – which signals confidence in its long‑term plan.
Its addition to the FTSE Global Equity Index Series is a liquidity boost that could attract passive funds.
There is no fresh operational news; the focus is on governance and index inclusion rather than new contracts or product launches.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – investors are waiting for a catalyst, not reacting yet.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A few posts highlight the dividend payout and the company’s link to a major operator’s valuation, suggesting a solid cash‑flow base.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Some chatter points to a recent share cancellation that could dilute existing holders and hints that the price may still be too low for a quick rebound.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has moved from cautious curiosity in early 2026 to a calm, waiting posture after the index inclusion announcement.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, meaning no one is searching for the ticker. This is a classic contrarian green light.

near_term_catalysts: The upcoming ex‑dividend date could trigger a short‑term rally as investors pile in to capture the payout.
Index rebalancing activity in late summer may bring new buy orders from passive funds.
Any announcement of a new long‑term contract with a major operator would give the market a clear upside story.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden spike in trading volume that breaks the current support level, or a press release announcing a fresh contract or partnership.
expect_next.timing_regime: The share appears to be in the early stage of a recovery cycle – the price has already fallen sharply, so a mean‑reversion bounce is the most likely path if a catalyst arrives.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the price recovers to roughly the midpoint of its recent low, it could double from today’s level – a 100% upside that outweighs the 70% drawdown risk. The company’s dividend history and index inclusion provide a solid floor.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The price could stay below its current support if no new contracts materialise, and a further share cancellation might erode shareholder value. A worst‑case decline would push the stock even lower, but it would still be a bargain relative to its assets.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: You could lose up to 70% of the invested capital if the stock keeps sliding, but a 100% gain would more than cover that risk. The asymmetry is clearly in favour of a contrarian play.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price touches the low of the recent range and volume spikes, setting a stop 10% below that low. Size the position so that a 70% loss would still leave you with a small cushion for a 100% upside.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see either a volume surge or a new contract announcement. Those are the signals that will confirm the mean‑reversion thesis.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risks have surfaced, so the position remains viable.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Zegona sits at a deep crash, is quiet on social media, and could bounce sharply once a dividend or new contract hits – a classic contrarian upside play.
